content: Looks like if there is an orphaned block prysm just decides to miss a few blocks afterwards

author: potuz
content: Hmm not sure what are the tests you're running but that seems like a Payload id cache miss. I'm testing these paths actively these days so can't confirm your finding now yet. When I reorg a node Prysm seems to handle it correctly here.

author: potuz
content: Oh which branch are you using? Given the time of these messages I guess this was Terence's rebasing of epbs-devnet-0, need to check if that has my latest `lateBlockTasks` PR cc <@363800010518822915>

author: potuz
content:
Hmmm, <@412614104222531604> can't reproduce this, how did you create the orphan? was it an orphaned block or a missed block? who created? was it Prysm orphaning a node that created that log or was it the next node?

At any rate, I'm not working on testing orphaning payloads and blocks and so far so good, on Prysm only devnets

author: barnabasbusa
content: I didn't create it on purpose

author: potuz
content: the log comes from whom? was it actually fully orphaned? anyway, too many variables now to try to reproduce, but for orphans when the block is arriving late, I think without this PR https://github.com/OffchainLabs/prysm/pull/16454 (which I'm not sure if it's in the branch you tested) I'd expect a payload cache miss which is what happens there.
